title: Revenue Operations Manager (formalize people-management) — Ledgerly
fitRationale: You were promoted to Senior Analyst with team-lead scope but never got a manager title; you’ve mentored two junior analysts, owned a three-person workstream and the executive revenue dashboard used in board meetings, and redesigned the quarterly forecasting model (variance down from 18% to 7%). Those are exactly the delivery and people signals Ledgerly needs to justify a manager slot.
upskills: Run repeatable people processes: one-on-ones, performance reviews and career plans for direct reports.
Manager training (conflict resolution, delegation frameworks) — complete one practical course and apply it to your team.
Convert your ad-hoc workstream into a staffed charter with KPIs and SLAs (document roles and capacity).
firstMove: This month: create a one-page promotion packet (scope, direct reports, KPIs, 3 proof points — include the 18%→7% forecast stat and the board dashboard), then ask your manager for a 30-minute career conversation to propose a formal manager role.

ambitionCheck.note: You already run people-like responsibilities (mentoring, leading a 3-person workstream) and own exec-facing metrics — moving to a manager title at Ledgerly is a reasonable next step.

title: Senior Revenue Strategy Lead (GTM & forecasting owner) — Ledgerly
fitRationale: You redesigned forecasting and improved accuracy substantially, handle commission design and stakeholder management, and maintain the board-facing revenue dashboard — that mix of modelling, GTM ops and executive storytelling fits a revenue-strategy lead role within Ledgerly’s fintech/B2B payments context.
upskills: Build a 6–12 month GTM strategy playbook (link forecasts to quota motion and commission design).
Deepen SQL-based cohort and ARR modelling to run scenario analysis for pricing and retention.
Practice concise board narratives: prepare 10-slide executive decks tying forecast drivers to revenue interventions.
firstMove: This month: draft a one-page ‘‘GTM forecast improvement’’ proposal (objective, impact metric, data sources: Salesforce/NetSuite, stakeholders) and volunteer to lead it with a clear 3-month milestone plan.

ambitionCheck.note: You already own forecasting, commission design and the exec dashboard — those are the core deliverables of a revenue-strategy lead, so this is a natural step up within Ledgerly.

title: Senior FP&A Partner — Revenue (internal move to Finance) — Ledgerly
fitRationale: You have NetSuite experience, financial modeling, forecasting chops and commission knowledge, plus a proven forecast accuracy improvement — those are directly transferable to an FP&A partner focused on revenue at Ledgerly and will broaden your influence inside Finance.
upskills: Formalize accounting/FP&A fundamentals (monthly close, variance analysis, IFRS basics) via a short course or shadowing.
Build repeatable NetSuite month-to-month models and automated reporting templates.
Strengthen cross-functional partnering with Finance and Sales on budgeting and quarter-end reconciliations.
firstMove: This month: set a 30-minute meeting with Ledgerly’s FP&A lead; present a one-page case showing how your forecasting model cut variance and propose a 30-day shadow/project to help month-end close or budget build.

ambitionCheck.note: You already use NetSuite and financial models and have demonstrable forecast impact — switching to an FP&A partner role at Ledgerly is realistic and leverages your strengths.
